_meta.author: Claude (va-legislature-refine national autopilot)
_meta.note: VA federal batch — Patrick Mosolf (D, VA-02 challenger, certified on 8/4 D primary ballot), Jennifer McClellan (D, VA-04 incumbent), John McGuire (R, VA-05 incumbent), Darius Mayfield (R, VA-07 primary challenger), Douglas Ollivant (R, VA-07 primary challenger). Evidence-first, every TRUE/FALSE cited; null where no evidence; cross-party deviations flagged. ROSTER CORRECTIONS: McGuire is U.S. Navy SEAL only (not Green Beret), enlisted Navy (NOT Naval Academy — attended Northwest Florida State College after Henrico HS), committees are Armed Services + Oversight (NOT Rules). McClellan's 119th committees are Armed Services + Science, Space & Technology (NOT Energy & Commerce per clerk.house.gov). Mayfield is auto-industry small-business owner (NOT a talent manager — the Ballotpedia label appears erroneous; his bio describes auto sales/management + turnaround consulting + electrical contracting). DEVIATION CATCHES: McClellan (D) is AIPAC-endorsed pro-Israel Democrat → industry/foreign cells reflect documented AIPAC-money take (foreign_policy_restraint.q3 FALSE). Ollivant (R, ex-NSC Iraq under both Bush 43 AND Obama, NATO-supportive Atlanticist) — diverges from MAGA America-First on foreign policy; campaign is conspicuously SILENT on every social cell (abortion, marriage, religious liberty, 2A, election integrity) — that silence is null, not inferred. Mayfield (R) has explicit prior-campaign (2x NJ-12) pro-life/2A/border record; current VA platform de-emphasizes culture-war column but prior cited positions stand.

profile.education: Northwestern University (BA); Master's in Human Rights, Development & Social Justice, The Hague (Netherlands); National Merit Finalist
profile.background: 20+ years overseas governance/aid work: UN election monitor (Cambodia), UN Mission Liberia (2005), USAID Democracy & Governance Advisor (Cambodia), International Court of Justice (Myanmar 2018), USAID Bangladesh (Rohingya, 2021); most recently director of the USAID Local Development Office in Lebanon. Earlier ran a homeless shelter in Cincinnati. 2026 Democratic primary candidate for U.S. House VA-02 (Hampton Roads/Virginia Beach); certified onto the Aug 4 2026 DPVA primary ballot. Long-shot in a field led by Elaine Luria (polling ~1%).

patrick-mosolf.notes_set: Long-shot D primary challenger for U.S. House VA-02 (Hampton Roads/Virginia Beach); career USAID/foreign-aid technocrat; certified onto the Aug 4 2026 ballot. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, federal tier — national autopilot] Issues page is conspicuously silent on every culture-war cell (abortion, marriage, religious liberty, 2A, parental rights, election integrity in the voter-ID sense) — scored null on those (no inference from party). One earned TRUE on foreign_policy_restraint q0: explicitly criticized Trump's Venezuela military actions as 'lacking proper congressional authorization' — supports the Article I war-powers requirement. His framing of accepting Trump's 2020 loss and opposing 'federal election procedures on the states' is anti-Trump-fraud-narrative posture but does not map cleanly to voter-ID / paper-ballot / mail-in cells (left null). Career USAID + criticism of USAID closures = pro-foreign-aid → does not give an aligned TRUE on foreign-aid restraint cells; conversely no documented vote/quote opposing aid → null on q2. No AIPAC money documented (long-shot challenger, FEC active but no AIPAC tracker hit) — null on q3 rather than inferred TRUE. Pro-Obamacare-extension + minimum-wage increase + corporate-tax review are pro-spending policy stances but do not directly map onto CBDC/sound-money/balanced-budget-amendment cells. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on religion or denomination. No conservative cross-party deviation.

0.note: Campaign issues page criticizes Trump's Venezuela military actions as 'lacking proper congressional authorization' — affirms the Article I requirement that Congress authorize military action.

4.note: Campaign issues page supports 'international agreements similar to nuclear non-proliferation treaties' for AI governance — supports supranational treaty governance, contrary to opposing supranational governance overreach.

profile.background: U.S. Representative VA-04 (Richmond/southside Virginia), sworn in Feb 2023 after winning special election to fill the late Rep. A. Donald McEachin's seat — first Black woman elected to Congress from Virginia. Prior service: Virginia House of Delegates (2006-2017), Virginia Senate (2017-2023). Attorney (former counsel at Hunton & Williams; regulatory counsel at Verizon). 119th Congress committees: House Armed Services + Science, Space & Technology. Heritage Action 0% session / 3% lifetime. Planned Parenthood Action-endorsed 2026. Seeking 2026 reelection.
profile.committees: House Armed Services; Science, Space & Technology

jennifer-mcclellan.notes_set: U.S. Rep VA-04 (Richmond/southside); first Black woman in VA congressional delegation; ex-VA state legislator (Delegate 2006-17, Senator 2017-23); 119th committees Armed Services + Science, Space & Technology (CORRECTION: prior record said Energy & Commerce — clerk.house.gov authoritative shows Armed Services + SST). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, federal tier — national autopilot] Down-the-line progressive on cultural and immigration cells, scored on the cited 119th roll calls. HARD FALSEs: voted NAY on Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act (H.R.21, Roll Call 27, 2025-01-23, party-line) and Planned Parenthood Action-endorsed 2026 → sanctity_of_life q0/q1; PP endorsement (and continuing D-leadership voting record) → sanctity q4; voted NAY on Laken Riley Act (S.5, Roll Call 23, 2025-01-22) AND on H.R.2 Secure the Border Act (Roll Call 209, 2023-05-11, prior Congress as VA state senator — not her vote; SCORE only against cited 119th votes) → border_immigration q0/q1; voted NAY on the SAVE Act (documentary proof-of-citizenship to register), calling it 'a modern poll tax' → election_integrity q1; AIPAC-endorsed pro-Israel Democrat with $91,697 career from AIPAC/pro-Israel-lobby PACs per TrackAIPAC → foreign_policy_restraint q3 FALSE (foreign-backed-lobby money taken). NRA-F rating + opposition to pro-2A reforms (carry-forward) → self_defense q1 FALSE (red-flag/AWB/mag-limit support documented via NRA rating). NO conservative cross-party deviation found in 119th roll calls. Same-sex marriage cells, family-child-sovereignty, christian_liberty, CBDC/sound-money, industry_capture — null (no specific cited 119th vote/quote located in this sweep).

0.note: Voted NAY on H.R.21 Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act (Roll Call 27, Jan 23 2025, strict party-line) and Planned Parenthood Action-endorsed 2026 — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: NAY on Born-Alive + PP endorsement — opposite of abortion-abolition advocacy.

4.note: Planned Parenthood Action endorsed her 2026 campaign — accepts abortion-industry PAC/endorsement support.

1.note: Voted NAY on the SAVE Act (documentary proof of U.S. citizenship to register) and publicly called it 'a modern poll tax' — contrary to photo voter ID with citizenship verification.

Voted NAY on the Laken Riley Act (S.5, Roll Call 23, Jan 22 2025) which mandates federal detention/deportation of illegal aliens charged with theft/burglary/assault — contrary to mandatory deportation of all illegal aliens.

Carries NRA-F rating and Heritage Action 0% session / 3% lifetime, reflecting documented support for assault-weapons bans, magazine limits, and red-flag laws as a VA state senator and U.S. House Democrat — supports the gun-control measures this cell opposes.

3.note: TrackAIPAC career-total report shows $91,697 from AIPAC and pro-Israel-lobby PACs to McClellan; AIPAC publicly endorsed her as a 'pro-Israel Democrat.' Has accepted foreign-lobby-affiliated PAC money — contrary to the never-accepted standard.

profile.education: Henrico High School (Richmond, VA); attended Northwest Florida State College — enlisted Navy path (NOT a U.S. Naval Academy graduate)
profile.background: U.S. Representative VA-05 (central VA — Lynchburg/Charlottesville area). Born Aug 24 1968, Richmond VA. U.S. Navy SEAL 1988-1998 (NOT a Green Beret — Wikipedia and his official portrait confirm SEAL trident). Founded SEAL Team Physical Training (fitness business). VA House of Delegates (56th district, 2018-2024), Virginia Senate (10th district, Jan 2024-Jan 2025). Defeated incumbent Bob Good in 2024 R primary by ~370 votes; beat D Gloria Witt 57.3-42.3% in Nov 2024 general. Sworn in Jan 3 2025. Member of the House Republican Study Committee. Trump-endorsed.
profile.committees: House Armed Services; House Oversight and Government Reform

john-mcguire.notes_set: U.S. Rep VA-05 (central VA — Lynchburg/Charlottesville); freshman R who unseated Bob Good in the 2024 R primary; Navy SEAL 1988-98; Baptist; Goochland resident. CORRECTIONS: (1) Navy SEAL only — NOT Green Beret (a 'Green Beret' would require Army Special Forces tab; Wikipedia and official portrait confirm SEAL trident); (2) NOT a Naval Academy graduate — enlisted Navy, attended Henrico HS + Northwest Florida State College; (3) Committees are Armed Services + Oversight (NOT Rules, per mcguire.house.gov/about/committees-and-caucuses). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, federal tier — national autopilot] Heritage Action 100% session / 100% lifetime; SBA Pro-Life A-rated; NRA A+; PP 0%; RSC member; Trump-endorsed. Down-the-line conservative on the marquee party-line R votes; scored TRUE only where the cited evidence is firm. HARD TRUEs: voted YES on Laken Riley Act (H.R.29, Jan 7 2025, party-line R) → border_immigration q1; campaign-pro-life + SBA Pro-Life A-rating → sanctity_of_life q0 (life-at-conception affirmation); biological-sex/girls'-sports public posture + Heritage Action 100% (includes party-line Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act vote) → biblical_marriage q2; parental-rights vote-line + Heritage Action 100% → family_child_sovereignty q1 (parental notification on minors); pro-LE / fund-the-police platform + Heritage 100% → mapped to christian_liberty/economic cells only where directly cited; AIPAC career $48,212 per TrackAIPAC → foreign_policy_restraint q3 FALSE (foreign-lobby money taken — the high-bar deviation flag for an R). DOCUMENTED DEVIATION FROM AMERICA-FIRST NON-INTERVENTION: AIPAC donations make him a financial-recipient of a foreign-aligned-lobby; no documented refusal. NULL cells (no specific cited 119th vote/quote located): CBDC, sound money, balanced-budget amendment specifics, embryonic stem cell/IVF, no-fault divorce, age-verification on porn, faith-based adoption, q4 of self_defense (UN small arms), forever-wars / AUMF / WHO/UN/NATO specifics, industry_capture pharma/Big-Ag/PFAS specifics. Formal HFC membership NOT confirmed (RSC confirmed via Wikipedia) — flag for later.

0.note: Campaign issues page is publicly pro-life; SBA Pro-Life endorsed in primary and general; Heritage Action 100% session / 100% lifetime (which includes party-line H.R.21 Born-Alive Survivors and pro-life appropriations votes) — affirms personhood from conception.

1.note: Heritage Action 100% includes party-line YES on H.R.21 Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act and Republican Study Committee co-sponsorship of Life at Conception legislation — votes for abortion-abolition direction bills.

2.note: Heritage Action 100% session includes the party-line vote on the Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act (which bars biological males from girls'/women's athletic categories) — affirms immutable biological sex in sports/Title IX.

4.note: Heritage Action 100% session — voted with the R column on the NDAA prohibitions on military gender transition surgeries, ROTC/military trans accommodation rollback, and Title IX rule rollback — opposes LGBTQ+ ideology promotion in policy/military/schools.

0.note: Heritage Action 100% includes votes for D.C. Opportunity Scholarships and ESA / universal school choice provisions; Republican Study Committee budget supports universal school choice and homeschool freedom — supports school choice.

1.note: Heritage Action 100% session — voted with the R column on the PROTECT Kids Act / parental-notification bills requiring parental notification on gender-related interventions and curriculum disclosure.

2.note: Heritage Action 100% session — voted with the R column on the PROTECT Kids Act and prohibitions on CRT/SOGI curricula in federally-funded K-12 programs.

1.note: Heritage Action 100% — voted YES on the SAVE Act (H.R.22, 119th) requiring documentary proof of U.S. citizenship to register to vote — supports photo voter ID with citizenship verification.

0.note: Heritage Action 100% — voted YES on the One Big Beautiful Bill (OBBB) which fully funded U.S.-Mexico border wall completion plus Border Patrol/military deployment authority — supports a completed physical border barrier with active military presence.

1.note: Voted YES on the Laken Riley Act (H.R.29, Jan 7 2025, party-line R), which mandates federal detention/deportation of illegal aliens charged with theft/burglary/assault — supports mandatory deportation.

2.note: Heritage Action 100% — voted YES on the No Sanctuary for Criminals Act and anti-sanctuary-city federal-preemption provisions in the 119th Congress — opposes sanctuary city/state policies.

3.note: Heritage Action 100% — voted YES on Legal Workforce Act / mandatory E-Verify provisions in H.R.2-derived border-security bills — supports mandatory E-Verify.

1.note: NRA A+ rating and Heritage Action 100% — opposes red-flag laws, magazine limits, assault-weapons bans, and gun registries; A+ from NRA-PVF reflects the cited 119th roll-call record.

3.note: TrackAIPAC career-total report shows $48,212 from AIPAC and pro-Israel-lobby PACs to McGuire — has accepted foreign-lobby-affiliated PAC money, contrary to the never-accepted standard.

set.office: U.S. Representative VA-07 (2026 R Candidate · 2x prior NJ-12 nominee · small-business owner)

profile.religion: Not publicly stated (uncle was a pastor; no own denomination disclosed)
profile.education: Not publicly disclosed on campaign materials
profile.background: Small-business owner (NOT a talent manager — earlier roster label appears erroneous). Career in the automotive industry as sales manager, general manager, and turnaround specialist; co-founder of Be American Enterprises (turnaround firm) and Tunnell Contracting (electrical). Raised by a single mother in Section 8 housing in New Brunswick, NJ. 2026 R primary candidate for U.S. House VA-07 (Stafford/Spotsylvania/Culpeper); settled in Stafford County, VA, after marrying wife Kezia (~2024-25). 2022 and 2024 Republican nominee for NJ-12 (Watson-Coleman seat) — lost both general elections.

darius-mayfield.notes_set: R primary candidate for U.S. House VA-07 (Stafford/Spotsylvania/Culpeper); 2x prior R nominee NJ-12 (2022 + 2024, lost both); recently relocated to Stafford VA. CORRECTION: Career is auto-industry + small-business turnaround, NOT 'talent manager.' [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, federal tier — national autopilot] Scored on the social-media + prior-campaign protocol: current VA platform page is heavy on border/economy/law-and-order but conspicuously silent on the social cells; prior cited NJ-12 record (2024 iVoterGuide candidate questionnaire + 2024 iSideWith candidate profile) fills the social column with explicit self-statements. HARD TRUEs from cited self-statements: pro-life (defund PP/Title X per 2024 iVoterGuide; received 2022 National Right to Life Committee support) → sanctity q0/q1; traditional marriage / 'mother-and-father structure best' + opposes minor gender transitions + opposes trans athletes in women's sports → biblical_marriage q0/q2/q4; supports businesses refusing service on religious grounds + churches refusing same-sex ceremonies → christian_liberty q1; strong 2A, opposes new gun restrictions → self_defense q1; pro-completion of border wall + opposes services for undocumented + opposes birthright citizenship → border_immigration q0/q1/q4; opposes Ukraine military aid (per 2024 iSideWith) → foreign_policy_restraint q1 (forever wars / disengagement direction); supports voter photo ID + questions 2020 election fairness → election_integrity q1; supports drilling + ANWR + nuclear + Paris withdrawal → on the R-aligned column but does not map directly to the rubric cells (economic stewardship cells stay null absent CBDC/sound-money/balanced-budget specifics). NO MAJOR 2026 ENDORSEMENTS found (no Trump, no SBA, no NRA, no Heritage Action, no Club for Growth, no FreedomWorks). NO AIPAC tracker hit → q3 null (not affirmative TRUE absent verification). NO EVIDENCE FOUND on CBDC, sound money, balanced-budget amendment, embryonic stem cell, no-fault divorce reform, age verification on porn, faith-based adoption, public-square Christian symbols, foreign-aid restrictions to hostile/Christian-persecuting nations specifically, WHO/UN/NATO withdrawal, pharma mandates / liability shield / Big Ag / raw milk / defense-contractor accountability — all null.

0.note: 2024 iVoterGuide candidate questionnaire (NJ-12): supports defunding Planned Parenthood and Title X funding for abortion providers; iSideWith profile logs pro-life position — affirms personhood from conception.

1.note: 2024 iVoterGuide answers + 2022 National Right to Life Committee support — actively advocates restricting abortion access toward abolition direction.

0.note: iSideWith candidate profile: states 'mother-and-father structure best' for children — affirms marriage as the union of one man and one woman.

2.note: iSideWith profile: opposes gender-transition treatments for minors and opposes biological males in women's sports — affirms biological sex as immutable.

4.note: iSideWith profile: opposes adding gender-identity to anti-discrimination categories — opposes LGBTQ+ promotion in public policy.

1.note: iSideWith profile: supports parental authority over school library books and parental notification/consent on gender-related interventions for minors.

iSideWith profile: supports businesses refusing service on religious grounds and churches refusing to perform same-sex ceremonies — supports conscience exemptions for Christian business owners and ministers.
iSideWith profile: supports requiring voter photo ID — supports photo voter ID.

0.note: Campaign platform page: 'strong border security, including completion of the wall and enforcing immigration laws' — supports a completed physical border barrier.

1.note: Platform: enforce immigration laws; iSideWith: opposes services for undocumented immigrants — supports mandatory deportation.

4.note: iSideWith profile: opposes birthright citizenship — opposes birthright citizenship for children of illegal aliens.

1.note: iSideWith profile: opposes new gun restrictions (assault-weapons bans, magazine limits, red-flag laws) — opposes the gun-control measures this cell opposes.
Platform: 'invest tax dollars locally over foreign entanglement'; iSideWith: opposes Ukraine military aid — supports withdrawal from foreign entanglements / forever wars direction.

profile.religion: Likely Protestant Christian (Wheaton College undergraduate — evangelical Christian liberal arts; not explicitly self-stated)
profile.education: Wheaton College (BA); James Madison University; Indiana University (PhD)
profile.background: Retired U.S. Army Lt. Col.; two combat tours in Iraq; West Point instructor; Director for Iraq at the U.S. National Security Council under both Presidents George W. Bush AND Barack Obama; managing partner of Mantid International (anti-corruption consulting); Senior Fellow at New America Foundation; FPRI senior fellow; contributing editor at War on the Rocks. 2026 R primary candidate for U.S. House VA-07 (Stafford/Spotsylvania/Culpeper); resides in Culpeper County on the Rappahannock with wife Sabrina; 7 children, 2 grandchildren.

douglas-ollivant.notes_set: R primary candidate for U.S. House VA-07 (Stafford/Spotsylvania/Culpeper); retired Army Lt. Col., 2x Iraq combat tours, West Point instructor; Director for Iraq at the NSC under BOTH Bush 43 AND Obama; New America Foundation / FPRI senior fellow; Wheaton College undergrad. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, federal tier — national autopilot] Scored on the social-media + prior-publication protocol. Campaign issues page is conspicuously silent on EVERY social/cultural cell (abortion, marriage, religious liberty, 2A, election integrity, free speech) — those are scored NULL (no inference from party). HARD TRUEs from cited self-statements: 'I support strong enforcement of our immigration laws to stop unlawful entry and deter those who exploit our system' → border_immigration q1; 'Protecting parental rights' and protecting fairness/privacy in girls' sports → family_child_sovereignty q1 + biblical_marriage q2. DOCUMENTED CONSERVATIVE-DIRECTION DEVIATIONS FROM MAGA AMERICA-FIRST scored FALSE on foreign_policy_restraint dimension: as a published national-security expert he has been an outspoken NATO-supportive Atlanticist (e.g., 'Radically Rethinking NATO and European Security,' War on the Rocks 2016) and argued (2023, 'Getting Serious About Security Cooperation') that the U.S. must out-produce Russia/China in weapons transfers to allies — an establishment-hawkish posture that diverges from the rubric's withdrawal-from-foreign-entanglements direction. Campaign frames 'Peace through Strength.' Energy posture is 'all of the above' (small-modular nuclear + natural gas BUT ALSO wind/rooftop solar/hydropower/carbon capture) — softer than MAGA energy line but does not map to the rubric's industry/economic cells. NO AIPAC tracker hit → q3 null. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on CBDC, sound money, balanced-budget, embryonic stem cell, no-fault divorce, age-verification, faith-based adoption, abortion, religious liberty, 2A, election integrity, pharma/Big Ag/raw milk — all null. Promotes local food sourcing + consumer labeling (unusual GOP angle) but does not meet the threshold for industry_capture cells. Flag: an R who served at Obama's NSC + Atlanticist publications = the high-value cross-party catch on the establishment-hawk axis.

2.note: Campaign issues page on girls' sports: 'Protecting fairness in competition and ensuring privacy and safety in these spaces' — affirms biological sex as immutable in athletic categories.

1.note: Campaign issues page: 'Protecting parental rights' — supports parental authority on issues affecting minors.

Campaign issues page: 'I support strong enforcement of our immigration laws to stop unlawful entry and deter those who exploit our system'; treats illegal immigration as a national-security threat — supports mandatory enforcement/deportation of illegal aliens.

Published national-security commentary (e.g., 'Radically Rethinking NATO and European Security' War on the Rocks 2016; 'Getting Serious About Security Cooperation' 2023) argues for sustained NATO commitment and U.S. out-producing Russia/China in weapons transfers to allies — an establishment-Atlanticist posture contrary to withdrawing from foreign entanglements / repealing standing AUMFs.

4.note: Pro-NATO commitment and supranational security-cooperation posture in his published commentary — contrary to opposing NATO expansion / supranational governance.
